item.title: My fiancé up and fucking died.
item.description: I just need to vent this and I dont want to make my friends and family nervous. My fiance died and I can't stop thinking about the life we're both missing out on now. We were going to buy a house this month. We were talking about finally having space and money to maybe have kids. We were supposed to be at Disney this week. I told him to wait till we had been dating 4 years before he proposed because I'd read there was a noted uptick in breakups around 4 years when people begin to realize the things that are permanent about a person. But after 4 years, I couldn't find anything about him that I didn't like. We'd only been engaged for a year. Why rush? I knew I wanted him he knew he wanted me. We weren't going anywhere. He would scoop up every bug in the house and put it outside. Even the ones he was really, deeply grossed out by. Spiders, moths, houseflies, those centipedes with the awful long legs. Even as his hands shook from being near the thing, he wouldn't kill it. Just catch it under a cup with piece of mail. This man was a Disney princess. Wild animals would come up to him. Birds would land on him. Stranger's children would come up and hold his hand and not even be alarmed to realize he was a stranger. I've never met someone with an aura. But he had one. And it made people feel safe. And it confused him a lot. Because he had anxiety often. And he made me feel strong. Plenty of dudes are happy to be your defender but he built me up so I could defend myself. He told me once "the stupidest thing anyone could do is underestimate you." And I told him that was the sexiest thing a man has ever said to me. Because it was. I was with a guy who made me feel like trash before him. That guy pushed me at a party once because he was drunk and insecure. My Fiance told me then, when we were just friends, that wasn't okay. Not that my bf was a monster or an asshole or I should leave him. I could argue with all of that. I couldn't argue with "that wasn't okay." Because it wasn't okay. He was my DnD DM first. He spun beautiful stories. Voices and puns and heartbreak and backstories that come back later. Varying cultures and history and maps and props. He was writing a dual campaign that would require 2 DMs and a huge group so the 2 groups could decide to fight or cooperate. He loved strategy board games that took 7 hours to play. He loved Star Trek and inventing gods and arguing philosophy but not in that annoying way where he just quotes other people's ideas that are supposed to be impressive. He had thoughts of his own that were complex and kind and sometimes dark with despair about how best intentions don't line up with real world ability. His despair was beautiful too. He'd say we should pray no God exists because, by evidence of existence, if it is not all random chance and cause and effect, God is either not kind or not competent. But humans at least want to be kind, we just get scared. When we disagreed, we knew how to respect each other anyway. Exactly once we had a real fight and we figured it out that night. I came out of the shower one day and he was just dead. He wasn't sick. He hadn't been injured or poisoned or overdosed. He didn't have chest pains. It's been a month and we haven't heard anything from the Coroner. I'm starting to worry all I'm going to get back are his ashes and a shrug. We had everything and then it disappeared. Maybe it's not cool to stunt on other widows or whatever but I mean we were *happier* than the average couple. We didn't fight AND we still were able to talk about important shit without being rude AND our sex life was great and getting better AND our savings plan was working. Part of me thinks about that scene in the princess bride, "you truly loved each other, so you might have even been truly happy. Not one couple in a thousand has that, not one. Why should you?" This will get lost in usersub and I want it to. I just need to put down the impressive, complex person he was. His family have very specific religious views and it keeps them from learning about who he really was. I dont know why, but that kills me. People should know who he was. And how fucking stupid it is that he just up and died all because I was in the shower for 5 minutes longer than he was. I want to die. But that won't bring him back and we were never people who believed we'd see each other when we died. So I'll muddle through. Because I promised him once that I would and because someone needs to tell people who he was. Edit: what did I fucking say about this dying in usersub, people???
item.dc:creator: KatieIsALady

item.title: "Animal rights" activists in Paris violently steal a homeless man's puppy
item.description: This is horrible, and even worse is their justification for having done such a heinous thing: "they alleged that the homeless person is a Roma [Gypsy] and that the animal was drugged, hadn’t been vaccinated and did not have an ID, and was malnourished.” But the better news is that the asshole activists were charged and the homeless man and his puppy were reunited. https://tamarindoyam.com/stolen-dog-returned-safely-with-broken-hearted-homeless-man/
item.dc:creator: Hauntologist2

item.title: Day 303: of cheeseburgers I want inside of me
item.description: Burger Teriyaki Beef Patty, Teriyaki Sauce, Roy Berger Cheese, Pepper-dried Bacon, Soft Onions & Mushrooms, Salad, Semi-dried Tomatoes, Pickled Onions & Cucumber On A Brioche Bun From Juliette Café & Brasserie In Aarhus, Denmark Previous Day 302: https://imgur.com/gallery/HX9yU4y MESSAGE ME BURGER PICS OR YOUR FAVORITE BURGER SPOTS! Map of all burger spots that are available on yelp! Big thanks to @OhBoyDoIHaveNews4U for this. Link to his post and map https://imgur.com/gallery/vcObDuD
item.dc:creator: crypto

item.title: In 2002 scientist/teacher Grover Krantz died and donated his body for study at a body farm. His only condition was that he be reunited with his beloved dog Clyde (that'd died years earlier) after the study was complete.Their new home is the Smithsonian.
item.dc:creator: TheRainbowegoSweet007

item.title: W.E. to replace Russian vac tubes with plant in GA
item.description: For those out of the know, guitarists (still) like vacuum tube based amplifiers. They are also used in a few other applications by preference (hi-fi audio) or in places like radio transmitters where nothing else can do the job. One of the few remaining manufacturing plants in the world was in Russia - a relic of Soviet Russia's antiquated military that still used them in much of their hardware until fairly recently. One other plant was in China but it closed four years ago to relocate and hasn't been seen since. There's only one other plant, in Slovakia. The Ukraine/Russian boycott cut off the Russian tubes from the market. Western Electric conducted a survey of guitarists to gauge interest in them manufacturing tubes. It took only a week of survey time for them to conclude it was a go. They have announced that they will be building the tubes at their site in Georgia, USA. They've pledged not to gouge on the tube prices, so tube users and manufacturers are hopeful that this works for everyone involved. https://www.guitarworld.com/news/western-electric-american-guitar-amp-tubes
item.dc:creator: RetrogradeLlama
